root_text.an3.84:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an3.84:1.2: Atha kho aññataro vajjiputtako bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so vajjiputtako bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.84:1.5: “Sakkhissasi pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, tīsu sikkhāsu sikkhituṁ—
root_text.an3.84:2.1: Yato kho tvaṁ, bhikkhu, adhisīlampi sikkhissasi, adhicittampi sikkhissasi, adhipaññampi sikkhissasi, tassa tuyhaṁ bhikkhu adhisīlampi sikkhato adhicittampi sikkhato adhipaññampi sikkhato rāgo pahīyissati, doso pahīyissati, moho pahīyissati.
root_text.an3.84:2.2: So tvaṁ rāgassa pahānā dosassa pahānā mohassa pahānā yaṁ akusalaṁ na taṁ karissasi, yaṁ pāpaṁ na taṁ sevissasī”ti.
root_text.an3.84:3.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu aparena samayena adhisīlampi sikkhi, adhicittampi sikkhi, adhipaññampi sikkhi.
root_text.an3.84:3.2: Tassa adhisīlampi sikkhato adhicittampi sikkhato adhipaññampi sikkhato rāgo pahīyi, doso pahīyi, moho pahīyi.
root_text.an3.84:3.3: So rāgassa pahānā dosassa pahānā mohassa pahānā yaṁ akusalaṁ taṁ nākāsi, yaṁ pāpaṁ taṁ na sevīti.

translation_text.an3.84:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an3.84:1.2: Then a certain Vajji monk went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an3.84:1.3: “Sir, each fortnight over a hundred and fifty training rules are recited.
translation_text.an3.84:1.4: I’m not able to train in them.”
translation_text.an3.84:1.5: “But monk, are you able to train in three trainings:
translation_text.an3.84:1.6: the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom?”
translation_text.an3.84:1.9: “So, monk, you should train in these three trainings:
translation_text.an3.84:1.10: the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.
translation_text.an3.84:2.1: As you train in these, you will give up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.84:2.2: Then you won’t do anything unskillful, or practice anything bad.”
translation_text.an3.84:3.1: After some time that monk trained in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.
translation_text.an3.84:3.2: He gave up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.84:3.3: Then he didn’t do anything unskillful, or practice anything bad.
